Our AI-native platform of technology, tools, and teams is fully integrated and purpose-built to power pioneers' most complex workloads.

https://samexpert.com/microsoft-copilot-.title: Microsoft's Copilot Reorganisation
https://samexpert.com/microsoft-copilot-.text:
URL Source: https://samexpert.com/microsoft-copilot-reorganisation-march-2026/

Published Time: 2026-03-27T17:25:34.612Z

Markdown Content:
## Microsoft's Copilot Reorganisation

On [17 March 2026](https://blogs.microsoft.com/blog/2026/03/17/announcing-copilot-leadership-update/), Satya Nadella announced the most significant restructuring of Microsoft's AI organisation since the company [hired Mustafa Suleyman](https://blogs.microsoft.com/blog/2024/03/19/mustafa-suleyman-deepmind-and-inflection-co-founder-joins-microsoft-to-lead-copilot/) two years earlier. Consumer and commercial Copilot were unified under one leadership team. Suleyman stepped back from day-to-day product leadership to focus on building in-house AI models, and a new generation of executives took the reins. Five days before that, Rajesh Jha, the Executive Vice President (EVP) who had led Microsoft's Experiences + Devices division for years, [announced his retirement](https://blogs.microsoft.com/blog/2026/03/12/microsoft-announces-experiences-devices-leadership-changes/) after 35 years at the company.

Illustration: SAMexpert

These are structural changes to how Microsoft builds and delivers AI. They arrived against a backdrop of mixed adoption data, a tech stock downturn, [$37.5 billion in quarterly AI capital expenditure](https://www.theregister.com/2026/02/02/microsoft_ai_spend_copilot/), and a [partnership with OpenAI](https://blogs.microsoft.com/blog/2025/10/28/the-next-chapter-of-the-microsoft-openai-partnership/) that has evolved from exclusivity towards mutual independence through four publicly renegotiated stages. Here is what happened, who the new leaders are, what their track records tell us about the direction ahead, and what it all means for the organisations that depend on Microsoft's products every day.

## Two separate announcements, five days apart

On [12 March 2026](https://blogs.microsoft.com/blog/2026/03/12/microsoft-announces-experiences-devices-leadership-changes/), Microsoft announced that Rajesh Jha, Executive Vice President of Experiences + Devices, would retire after 35 years at the company. He will step down on 1 July 2026 and stay on in an advisory role. Jha had led the Experiences + Devices division responsible for [Microsoft 365 Copilot](https://samexpert.com/microsoft-365-copilot-licensing/), Windows, and Office. His responsibilities were divided among four EVPs reporting directly to Nadella: Perry Clarke, Charles Lamanna, Pavan Davuluri, and Ryan Roslansky. Jeff Teper was promoted to EVP, and Sumit Chauhan and Kirk Koenigsbauer were promoted to President. Jha's memo said the full cascade of organisational details would be finalised between March and June, ready for the start of FY27 (Microsoft's fiscal year 2027, beginning 1 July 2026).

https://docs.x.ai/developers/release-not.title: Release Notes
https://docs.x.ai/developers/release-not.text:
URL Source: https://docs.x.ai/developers/release-notes

Published Time: 2026-04-08T00:00:00Z

Markdown Content:
#### [Release Notes](https://docs.x.ai/developers/release-notes#release-notes)

Stay up to date with the latest changes to the xAI API.

* * *

## [March 2026](https://docs.x.ai/developers/release-notes#march-2026)

Mar 15

### [Batch API supports Image and Video generation](https://docs.x.ai/developers/release-notes#batch-api-supports-image-and-video-generation)

The [Batch API](https://docs.x.ai/developers/advanced-api-usage/batch-api) now supports [image generation](https://docs.x.ai/developers/model-capabilities/images/generation), [image editing](https://docs.x.ai/developers/model-capabilities/images/generation), and [video generation](https://docs.x.ai/developers/model-capabilities/video/generation) in addition to chat completions. Both [server-side tools](https://docs.x.ai/developers/tools/overview) and client-side function tools are also now supported in batch requests. Image and video URLs in batch results expire after 1 hour.

### [Batch API JSONL file upload](https://docs.x.ai/developers/release-notes#batch-api-jsonl-file-upload)

You can now create batches by uploading a [JSONL file](https://docs.x.ai/developers/advanced-api-usage/batch-api#jsonl-file-upload) via the Files API. Supports all batch endpoints including chat, image, and video in a single file.

Mar 12

### [Provisioned Throughput is available](https://docs.x.ai/developers/release-notes#provisioned-throughput-is-available)

Enterprise customers can now purchase dedicated API capacity with guaranteed tokens per minute. [Learn more](https://docs.x.ai/developers/provisioned-throughput).

Mar 10

### [Grok 4.20 and Grok 4.20 Multi-agent are live](https://docs.x.ai/developers/release-notes#grok-420-and-grok-420-multi-agent-are-live)

*   For more details on Grok 4.20 Multi-agent, check out the [docs](https://docs.x.ai/developers/model-capabilities/text/multi-agent)

* * *

## [January 2026](https://docs.x.ai/developers/release-notes#january-2026)

Jan 28

### [Video Generation & Next-Gen Image Generation](https://docs.x.ai/developers/release-notes#video-generation--next-gen-image-generation)

[Video Generation](https://docs.x.ai/developers/model-capabilities/video/generation) and a revamped [Image Generation](https://docs.x.ai/developers/model-capabilities/images/generation) are now available.

Jan 28

### [Batch API is released](https://docs.x.ai/developers/release-notes#batch-api-is-released)

[Batch API](https://docs.x.ai/developers/advanced-api-usage/batch-api) is available for all customers. It enables efficient batch processing of multiple requests, providing a better experience for users who need to submit large volumes of requests at once.

* * *

## [December 2025](https://docs.x.ai/developers/release-notes#december-2025)

Dec 16

### [Grok Voice Agent API is released](https://docs.x.ai/developers/release-notes#grok-voice-agent-api-is-released)

https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/machine-lea.title: Introducing agent-to-agent protocol support in Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime | Amazon Web Services
https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/machine-lea.text:
URL Source: https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/machine-learning/introducing-agent-to-agent-protocol-support-in-amazon-bedrock-agentcore-runtime/

Published Time: 2025-11-11T13:32:31-08:00

Markdown Content:
We recently announced the support for Agent-to-Agent (A2A) protocol on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime. With this addition, agents can discover peers, share capabilities, and coordinate actions across platforms using standardized communication.

Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime provides a secure, serverless environment designed for deploying AI agents and tools. It works with [any framework](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/devguide/using-any-agent-framework.html) and [model](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/devguide/using-any-model.html), supports [real-time](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/devguide/response-streaming.html)and [long-running workloads](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/devguide/runtime-long-run.html), and supports[session isolation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/devguide/runtime-sessions.html) with [built-in authentication](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/devguide/runtime-oauth.html). With support for [MCP](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/devguide/runtime-mcp.html), and now the[A2A protocol](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/devguide/runtime-a2a.html), Bedrock AgentCore Runtime enables seamless communication between agents. Agents built using different frameworks, [Strands Agents](https://strandsagents.com/latest/), [OpenAI Agents SDK](https://openai.github.io/openai-agents-python/), [LangGraph](https://www.langchain.com/langgraph),[Google ADK](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/), or [Claude Agents SDK](https://docs.claude.com/en/api/agent-sdk/overview), can share context, capabilities, and reasoning in a common, verifiable format.

In this post, we demonstrate how you can use the A2A protocol for AI agents built with different frameworks to collaborate seamlessly. You’ll learn how to deploy A2A servers on AgentCore Runtime, configure agent discovery and authentication, and build a real-world multi-agent system for incident response. We’ll cover the complete A2A request lifecycle, from agent card discovery to task delegation, showing how standardized protocols eliminate the complexity of multi-agent coordination.

## Understanding multi-agent systems

https://www.nxcode.io/resources/news/gro.text:
URL Source: https://www.nxcode.io/resources/news/grok-5-release-date-latest-news-2026

Published Time: 2026-03-24T00:00:00.000Z

Markdown Content:
## The Latest on Grok 5 (March 2026 Update)

Grok 5 has not been released as of March 2026. [Elon Musk originally confirmed](https://phemex.com/news/article/elon-musk-confirms-grok-5-launch-for-q1-2026-36075) a Q1 2026 launch, but that window has passed. xAI's official X account now points to **Q2 2026** as the most likely release window. The model carries a reported [6 trillion parameters](https://news.aibase.com/news/22865) using a Mixture-of-Experts architecture and is training on xAI's [1-gigawatt Colossus 2 supercluster](https://www.teslarati.com/elon-musk-xai-brings-1gw-colossus-2-ai-training-cluster-online/) in Memphis, Tennessee. For context, its predecessor [Grok 4 scored 88% on GPQA Diamond](https://x.ai/news/grok-4) and 25.4% on Humanity's Last Exam.

Here is what has happened in the last 30 days:

*   **March 3, 2026** -- xAI shipped Grok 4.20 Beta 2 with five targeted fixes: better instruction following, fewer hallucinations, enhanced LaTeX support, more accurate image search, and improved multi-image rendering. This is the current publicly available model, not Grok 5.
*   **February 25, 2026** -- Grok's official X account updated its projection, pointing to **Q2 2026** as the most likely window for Grok 5's full release.
*   **February 17-18, 2026** -- Grok 4.20 Beta launched with a 4-agent system, followed immediately by Grok 4.20 Heavy with 16 specialized agents. These multi-agent architectures are widely viewed as stepping stones toward Grok 5.
*   **January 2026** -- xAI confirmed Colossus 2, its 1-gigawatt supercluster in Memphis, Tennessee, is fully operational and actively training Grok 5.

The bottom line: Grok 5 missed its Q1 deadline. The new consensus points to Q2 2026.

* * *

## Expected Timeline: When Is Grok 5 Coming Out?

Based on all available signals, here is the most likely timeline:

| Milestone | Expected Window |
| --- | --- |
| Training completion | April 2026 |
| Internal testing / red-teaming | April-May 2026 |
| Public beta (X Premium+) | May-June 2026 |
| Full API access | Q3 2026 |

**Why Q2?** Three key factors point to this window:

1.   **Colossus 2 upgrade to 1.5GW in April**: Musk confirmed that the supercluster is expanding from 1GW to 1.5GW by April 2026. This upgrade aligns with the completion of Grok 5's primary training run -- the additional capacity would support fine-tuning and inference at scale.

2.   **Polymarket odds**: The prediction market gives a 33% probability that Grok 5 ships by June 30, 2026, and only 1% by March 31. The smart money says Q2.

https://coey.com/resources/blog/2026/02/.title: Claude Sonnet 4.6: The 1M Token Upgrade That Turns “Chat” Into Ops
https://coey.com/resources/blog/2026/02/.text:
URL Source: https://coey.com/resources/blog/2026/02/17/claude-sonnet-4-6-the-1m-token-upgrade-that-turns-chat-into-ops/

Published Time: 2026-02-17T22:48:05+00:00

Markdown Content:
Anthropic just dropped [Claude Sonnet 4.6](https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-sonnet-4-6), and it’s the kind of release that matters less for leaderboard flexing and more for the unglamorous stuff that makes AI actually usable: **long context, more reliable multi-step execution, stronger computer use, and better extraction-style workflows**.

Yes, the headline is the **1M token context window (beta)**. But the real story is that Anthropic keeps pushing Sonnet toward the default workhorse model lane: **good enough to run daily, cheap enough to scale, and structured enough to plug into automation**. That’s the sweet spot for executives and marketing operators who don’t need a sci-fi oracle. They need a machine collaborator that reduces rework.

> The shift isn’t “AI writes better.” It’s **AI holds the whole project in its head** long enough to stop breaking your workflow into a thousand copy paste rituals.

## What actually changed in Sonnet 4.6

Anthropic frames Sonnet 4.6 as a practical upgrade across coding, tool use, and long-context work. The main additions cluster into four “this helps your pipeline” areas.

### **1M token context (beta)**

Sonnet 4.6 can ingest up to **one million tokens** of context in a single request, **in beta**, and not automatically available to everyone. Anthropic’s docs spell out how context windows work and how extended context is gated, including the beta header requirement for 1M context ([context window documentation](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/context-windows)).

Translation for non-technical leaders: you can pilot it, but don’t assume every workflow and account can suddenly throw an entire company Dropbox at the model.

Also, long context does not mean long attention. It means the model can access more. You still need structure, schemas, critics, and retrieval hygiene, otherwise you’ll just get **beautifully written confusion at scale**.

### **More dependable multi-step execution**

The less meme-worthy upgrade is **planning and follow-through**. Sonnet 4.6 is positioned as better at handling multi-step instructions without dropping steps or creatively reinterpreting requirements. That matters for any automation that looks like:

*   read → extract → transform → validate → package → handoff
*   or “do the task, then do three more tasks, then output in a strict format”

In creator and marketing ops, that’s basically every day that ends in “please format this for the CMS, ESP, deck, or report.”

### **Stronger coding and computer use reliability**
